_comment: Loaded by .claude/hooks/validate_commit_message.py at every git commit -m and
1. .chglog/config.yml (subject parsing, allowed types, BREAKING CHANGE keyword)
2. docs_dev/style/cpp_style.md § Git commit messages (body shape: trivial-
vs-substantial, two-section pattern, file-list bullet format, language
- header.pattern: ^(\w*)\:\s(.*)$        (type and subject only — NO scope)
- Trivial commits (typos, exec-bit, formatting-only, dependency bumps) ship
with no body. Substantial commits use a two-section body:
(a) Context paragraphs explaining why, with backtick'd symbols/flags/files;
(b) File-list bullets in the form `- path: change`, optionally grouped under
Brace shorthand `name.{h,cpp}` is the convention for header/source pairs.
_body_comment: Body shape rules implement the two-section pattern from
docs_dev/style/cpp_style.md § Git commit messages.
Body-required policy is keyed off two signals, both regardless of commit type:
1. Subject pattern. If the subject matches one of the trivial_commit_subject
patterns (typos, exec-bit, format, dep bumps), the body is optional.
2. Diff size. If insertions + deletions in the staged diff fall at or below
small_diff_line_threshold, missing body is a soft-warn rather than hard-block.
For `git commit -am ...`, unstaged-tracked changes are also counted, since
`-a` would stage them at commit time.
The combinations:
trivial subject + small diff   + no body → silent pass (the typical case)
trivial subject + large diff   + no body → soft-warn ('trivial subject but
the diff is N lines — double check')
non-trivial    + small diff   + no body → soft-warn ('consider adding a body')
non-trivial    + large diff   + no body → HARD BLOCK
any            + body present + any     → run body-shape checks
When a body IS present and contains a file-list section, the validator checks
that file-list lines match `file_list_bullet_pattern`. The shape check is soft-
warn rather than hard-block because the regex cannot perfectly recognize valid
prose.
examples_good: fix: preserve missing-value emission for ASMD on indels
chore: extract input-spec parser for N-sample case/control
chore: fix typo in --num-threads help text
examples_bad: Fixed the variant store deduplication issue.
fix(caller): scope is not allowed by .chglog/config.yml header pattern
refactor: refactor is not in the chglog filter list; use 'chore:' instead
docs: docs is not in the chglog filter list

examples_substantial_body.subject: fix: preserve missing-value emission for ASMD on indels
body: ASMD was emitting `0.0` for indel paths where the alt-allele soft-clip
subtraction underflowed past the reference length, producing a value
that downstream filters interpreted as a real (poor) alignment quality
score. The correct emission is `.` (untestable) per `vcf_header_builder.cpp`.
The fix moves the underflow check to `compute_asmd` and treats any
negative intermediate as untestable rather than clamping to zero.
- src/lancet/caller/sample_format_data.cpp: skip ASMD on indel paths
- tests/caller/asmd_test.cpp: regression case for indel underflow

examples_substantial_body.subject: feat: add --probe-variants for known-truth diagnostics
body: not be exercised from the CLI; this exposes it via `--probe-variants <vcf>`,
loading the provided VCF, hashing each variant's k-mer set, and tracking
graph paths that recover those k-mers. The output goes to `<out>.probe.tsv`.
- src/lancet/core/probe_diagnostics.cpp: wire CLI input to probe runner
